meanings.definition: beliefs of a person or social group in which they have an emotional investment (either for or against something)
a numerical quantity measured or assigned or computed
the quality (positive or negative) that renders something desirable or valuable
examples: the Shakespearean Shylock is of dubious value in the modern world

meanings.definition: the amount (of money or goods or services) that is considered to be a fair equivalent for something else
examples: he tried to estimate the value of the produce at normal prices

meanings.definition: relative darkness or lightness of a color; -Joe Hing Lowe
examples: I establish the colors and principal values by organizing the painting into three values--dark, medium...and light

meanings.definition: (music) the relative duration of a musical note
an ideal accepted by some individual or group
fix or determine the value of; assign a value to
examples: value the jewelry and art work in the estate

meanings.definition: evaluate or estimate the nature, quality, ability, extent, or significance of
examples: I will have the family jewels appraised by a professional
access all the factors when taking a risk
How would you rate his chances to become President?
Gold was rated highly among the Romans
